name: Turtle Protein Cheesecake
description: Turtle protein cheesecake derived from the Vanilla Pumpkin base — pecan skipped, so caramel + chocolate carry the turtle identity. Adds a thin graham-forward chocolate crust (graham crumbs + cocoa, egg-white bound, minimal butter), a protein caramel swirl marbled into the filling (butterscotch SF pudding + 0% yogurt), and a top drizzle of REAL brown-sugar caramel + unsweetened-baking-chocolate ganache, finished with flaky salt. Filling protein bumped 2 -> 2.5 scoops to hold the macro ratio. Lands ~11.5 cal/g protein (~217 cal / 19 g protein per slice of 8). Splenda throughout, never monk fruit; the real caramel is the one honest-sugar indulgence, kept small. Bake in a 9-inch pan with a parchment sling for lift-out.

payload.instructionText: Once fully chilled (ideally overnight), lift the cake out with the parchment sling onto a plate. Warm the caramel and the ganache separately just until pourable — a few seconds; they should ribbon, not run. Drizzle both over the top in a back-and-forth criss-cross (use a spoon, or a zip-top bag with a tiny corner snipped). Finish with a pinch of flaky salt. Slice with a hot, wiped knife.

Pour half the filling onto the cooled crust. Drop spoonfuls of the protein caramel swirl over it, add the rest of the filling, then drag a knife through in a few S-strokes to marble (don't overmix). Tap the pan on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

payload.text: Mix on low after adding eggs — the 2.5 scoops of vanilla protein make this batter dense and prone to aeration. Overbeating here traps air that expands in the oven, then collapses and cracks the top.

Only 2-3 lazy S-strokes with the knife to marble the butterscotch swirl — more than that and you'll fully incorporate it, losing the pockets. The swirl should still be visible as distinct veins before it goes in the oven.

Pull at the first sign of a set edge with a slight wobble in the center (~155-160F) — with 2.5 scoops of protein this filling dries out faster than a traditional cheesecake. A fully still center means it's already overdone.

For a drizzle-consistency caramel (not thick), stop as soon as the almond milk is whisked smooth (~225F) and the sauce ribbons off a spoon — it thickens significantly as it cools, so pull earlier than you think you need to.

Add the almond milk WARM — cold liquid hits the melted unsweetened baking chocolate and can seize it into a grainy clump. A few seconds in the microwave before whisking it in prevents a seized ganache.
